---
name: data-storage
description: Patterns for persisting data using Plugin Data (node-level), Shared Plugin Data (cross-plugin), and Client Storage (user preferences). Use when storing metadata on nodes, syncing preferences, or implementing data migration.
---

# Data Storage in Figma Plugins

## Overview

Figma plugins have multiple storage mechanisms for persisting data:

1. **Plugin Data** - Stored on nodes, survives file operations
2. **Client Storage** - Persistent storage per user/device
3. **Shared Plugin Data** - Readable by other plugins

## Plugin Data (Node Storage)

### When to Use

- Store node-specific metadata
- Track plugin-generated nodes
- Store configuration per node
- Associate external IDs with Figma nodes

### Basic Operations

```typescript
// Write data
node.setPluginData("key", "value")

// Read data
const value = node.getPluginData("key")

// List all keys
const keys = node.getPluginDataKeys()

// Delete data (set to empty string)
node.setPluginData("key", "")
```

### Storing Complex Objects

```typescript
// Store object as JSON
const metadata = {
  createdAt: Date.now(),
  version: "1.0.0",
  author: "plugin",
  settings: {
    enabled: true,
    color: "#FF0000"
  }
}

node.setPluginData("metadata", JSON.stringify(metadata))

// Retrieve and parse
const storedData = node.getPluginData("metadata")
if (storedData) {
  const parsed = JSON.parse(storedData)
  console.log(parsed.version)
}
```

### Use Cases

#### Track Generated Nodes

```typescript
// Mark a node as generated by plugin
export const markAsGenerated = (node: SceneNode, config: any) => {
  node.setPluginData("generated", "true")
  node.setPluginData("generatedAt", Date.now().toString())
  node.setPluginData("config", JSON.stringify(config))
}

// Find all generated nodes
export const findGeneratedNodes = (): SceneNode[] => {
  return figma.currentPage.findAll(node => {
    return node.getPluginData("generated") === "true"
  })
}

// Check if node was generated
export const isGenerated = (node: SceneNode): boolean => {
  return node.getPluginData("generated") === "true"
}
```

#### Store External References

```typescript
// Link node to external database ID
export const linkToDatabase = (node: SceneNode, dbId: string) => {
  node.setPluginData("dbId", dbId)
  node.setPluginData("linkedAt", Date.now().toString())
}

// Get database ID from node
export const getDatabaseId = (node: SceneNode): string | null => {
  return node.getPluginData("dbId") || null
}

// Find node by database ID
export const findNodeByDbId = (dbId: string): SceneNode | null => {
  return figma.currentPage.findOne(node => {
    return node.getPluginData("dbId") === dbId
  })
}
```

#### Store Node State

```typescript
// Save node state
export const saveNodeState = (node: SceneNode, state: string) => {
  node.setPluginData("state", state)
  node.setPluginData("stateUpdatedAt", Date.now().toString())
}

// Enable/disable feature on node
export const setNodeFeature = (node: SceneNode, feature: string, enabled: boolean) => {
  const features = JSON.parse(node.getPluginData("features") || "{}")
  features[feature] = enabled
  node.setPluginData("features", JSON.stringify(features))
}

// Check if feature is enabled
export const isFeatureEnabled = (node: SceneNode, feature: string): boolean => {
  const features = JSON.parse(node.getPluginData("features") || "{}")
  return features[feature] === true
}
```

## Shared Plugin Data

### When to Use

- Share data with other plugins
- Store data that should be readable by team
- Standardized metadata (like component documentation)

### Operations

```typescript
// Write shared data (requires namespace)
node.setSharedPluginData("com.yourcompany.pluginname", "key", "value")

// Read shared data
const value = node.getSharedPluginData("com.yourcompany.pluginname", "key")

// List keys in namespace
const keys = node.getSharedPluginDataKeys("com.yourcompany.pluginname")
```

### Use Case: Component Documentation

```typescript
// Store component documentation
export const setComponentDocs = (component: ComponentNode, docs: {
  description: string
  props: { [key: string]: string }
  examples: string[]
}) => {
  const namespace = "com.yourcompany.documentation"
  component.setSharedPluginData(namespace, "description", docs.description)
  component.setSharedPluginData(namespace, "props", JSON.stringify(docs.props))
  component.setSharedPluginData(namespace, "examples", JSON.stringify(docs.examples))
}

// Read documentation
export const getComponentDocs = (component: ComponentNode) => {
  const namespace = "com.yourcompany.documentation"
  const description = component.getSharedPluginData(namespace, "description")
  const props = JSON.parse(component.getSharedPluginData(namespace, "props") || "{}")
  const examples = JSON.parse(component.getSharedPluginData(namespace, "examples") || "[]")
  
  return { description, props, examples }
}
```

## Client Storage

### When to Use

- User preferences
- Authentication tokens
- Recent files/history
- Plugin settings
- Cached data

### Basic Operations

```typescript
// Write data
await figma.clientStorage.setAsync("key", value)

// Read data
const value = await figma.clientStorage.getAsync("key")

// Delete data
await figma.clientStorage.deleteAsync("key")

// List all keys
const keys = await figma.clientStorage.keysAsync()
```

### Storage Limits

- Maximum of 1MB per key
- Maximum of 100 keys per plugin
- Data persists across files and plugin sessions

### Use Cases

#### User Preferences

```typescript
interface UserPreferences {
  theme: "light" | "dark"
  language: string
  autoSave: boolean
  lastUsedSettings: {
    color: string
    size: number
  }
}

// Save preferences
export const saveUserPreferences = async (prefs: UserPreferences) => {
  await figma.clientStorage.setAsync("userPreferences", prefs)
}

// Load preferences
export const loadUserPreferences = async (): Promise<UserPreferences | null> => {
  return await figma.clientStorage.getAsync("userPreferences")
}

// Update single preference
export const updatePreference = async <K extends keyof UserPreferences>(
  key: K,
  value: UserPreferences[K]
) => {
  const prefs = await loadUserPreferences() || {} as UserPreferences
  prefs[key] = value
  await saveUserPreferences(prefs)
}
```

#### Recent History

```typescript
interface HistoryItem {
  id: string
  timestamp: number
  action: string
  data: any
}

// Add to history
export const addToHistory = async (item: Omit<HistoryItem, "timestamp">) => {
  const history = await figma.clientStorage.getAsync("history") || []
  
  history.unshift({
    ...item,
    timestamp: Date.now()
  })
  
  // Keep only last 50 items
  const trimmed = history.slice(0, 50)
  
  await figma.clientStorage.setAsync("history", trimmed)
}

// Get history
export const getHistory = async (): Promise<HistoryItem[]> => {
  return await figma.clientStorage.getAsync("history") || []
}

// Clear history
export const clearHistory = async () => {
  await figma.clientStorage.deleteAsync("history")
}
```

#### Authentication State

```typescript
interface AuthState {
  token: string
  userId: string
  expiresAt: number
  refreshToken: string
}

// Save auth state
export const saveAuthState = async (auth: AuthState) => {
  await figma.clientStorage.setAsync("auth", auth)
}

// Load auth state
export const loadAuthState = async (): Promise<AuthState | null> => {
  return await figma.clientStorage.getAsync("auth")
}

// Check if authenticated
export const isAuthenticated = async (): Promise<boolean> => {
  const auth = await loadAuthState()
  if (!auth) return false
  
  // Check if token expired
  return auth.expiresAt > Date.now()
}

// Clear auth (logout)
export const clearAuthState = async () => {
  await figma.clientStorage.deleteAsync("auth")
}
```

#### Cache Management

```typescript
interface CacheEntry<T> {
  data: T
  timestamp: number
  expiresIn: number // milliseconds
}

// Set cached data
export const setCachedData = async <T>(
  key: string,
  data: T,
  expiresIn: number = 3600000 // 1 hour default
) => {
  const entry: CacheEntry<T> = {
    data,
    timestamp: Date.now(),
    expiresIn
  }
  await figma.clientStorage.setAsync(`cache_${key}`, entry)
}

// Get cached data (returns null if expired)
export const getCachedData = async <T>(key: string): Promise<T | null> => {
  const entry = await figma.clientStorage.getAsync(`cache_${key}`) as CacheEntry<T> | null
  
  if (!entry) return null
  
  // Check if expired
  const age = Date.now() - entry.timestamp
  if (age > entry.expiresIn) {
    await figma.clientStorage.deleteAsync(`cache_${key}`)
    return null
  }
  
  return entry.data
}

// Clear all cache
export const clearCache = async () => {
  const keys = await figma.clientStorage.keysAsync()
  const cacheKeys = keys.filter(key => key.startsWith("cache_"))
  
  for (const key of cacheKeys) {
    await figma.clientStorage.deleteAsync(key)
  }
}
```

## Data Migration

### Versioning

```typescript
interface PluginData {
  version: string
  data: any
}

// Save with version
export const saveVersionedData = async (key: string, data: any) => {
  const wrapped: PluginData = {
    version: "2.0.0",
    data
  }
  await figma.clientStorage.setAsync(key, wrapped)
}

// Load and migrate if needed
export const loadVersionedData = async (key: string): Promise<any> => {
  const stored = await figma.clientStorage.getAsync(key) as PluginData | null
  
  if (!stored) return null
  
  // Migrate old versions
  if (stored.version === "1.0.0") {
    const migrated = migrateV1toV2(stored.data)
    await saveVersionedData(key, migrated)
    return migrated
  }
  
  return stored.data
}

// Migration function
const migrateV1toV2 = (oldData: any): any => {
  // Transform old data structure to new one
  return {
    ...oldData,
    newField: "default value"
  }
}
```

## Best Practices

### Plugin Data

```typescript
// ✅ Use descriptive key names
node.setPluginData("export-settings", JSON.stringify(settings))

// ❌ Avoid generic names
node.setPluginData("data", JSON.stringify(settings))

// ✅ Namespace your keys
node.setPluginData("myplugin.feature.config", value)

// ✅ Clean up when deleting nodes
export const cleanupNode = (node: SceneNode) => {
  const keys = node.getPluginDataKeys()
  for (const key of keys) {
    node.setPluginData(key, "")
  }
}
```

### Client Storage

```typescript
// ✅ Handle missing data gracefully
const prefs = await figma.clientStorage.getAsync("prefs") || DEFAULT_PREFS

// ✅ Use try-catch for storage operations
try {
  await figma.clientStorage.setAsync("key", largeData)
} catch (error) {
  figma.notify("Failed to save: data too large")
}

// ✅ Compress large data
import { compress, decompress } from 'some-compression-library'

await figma.clientStorage.setAsync("key", compress(largeData))
const data = decompress(await figma.clientStorage.getAsync("key"))
```

### Performance

```typescript
// ✅ Batch reads when possible
const [prefs, history, auth] = await Promise.all([
  figma.clientStorage.getAsync("prefs"),
  figma.clientStorage.getAsync("history"),
  figma.clientStorage.getAsync("auth")
])

// ✅ Don't read in loops
const nodes = figma.currentPage.selection
const cache = new Map()

for (const node of nodes) {
  const id = node.getPluginData("id")
  if (!cache.has(id)) {
    // Fetch once per unique ID
    cache.set(id, await fetchDataById(id))
  }
}
```

## Common Patterns

### Sync State Between Storage Types

```typescript
// Save both node data and client storage
export const saveNodeWithBackup = async (node: SceneNode, data: any) => {
  // Save on node
  node.setPluginData("data", JSON.stringify(data))
  
  // Backup in client storage
  const backups = await figma.clientStorage.getAsync("nodeBackups") || {}
  backups[node.id] = data
  await figma.clientStorage.setAsync("nodeBackups", backups)
}

// Restore from backup if node data is missing
export const loadNodeWithFallback = async (node: SceneNode): Promise<any> => {
  let data = node.getPluginData("data")
  
  if (!data) {
    // Try to restore from backup
    const backups = await figma.clientStorage.getAsync("nodeBackups") || {}
    if (backups[node.id]) {
      data = JSON.stringify(backups[node.id])
      node.setPluginData("data", data)
    }
  }
  
  return data ? JSON.parse(data) : null
}
```

### Bulk Operations

```typescript
// Save data to multiple nodes efficiently
export const bulkSavePluginData = (
  nodes: SceneNode[],
  key: string,
  getValue: (node: SceneNode) => string
) => {
  for (const node of nodes) {
    node.setPluginData(key, getValue(node))
  }
}

// Read data from multiple nodes
export const bulkReadPluginData = (
  nodes: SceneNode[],
  key: string
): Map<string, string> => {
  const results = new Map()
  
  for (const node of nodes) {
    const value = node.getPluginData(key)
    if (value) {
      results.set(node.id, value)
    }
  }
  
  return results
}
```
